overrides.production_entry_point: fresh PyInstaller artifact -> Wisp executable -> frozen supervisor dispatch -> real UI/native/brain/audio workers
success_assertions: A freshly built packaged executable shows the real overlay, receives successful pings from four distinct worker processes, starts flows/hotkeys, exits cleanly, and leaves no managed process behind.
overrides.audit_note: The release build for Windows, Linux, and macOS now runs the same process-level smoke before packaging; the local Windows artifact was rebuilt and executed successfully.

overrides.production_entry_point: platform Start Wisp development launcher -> runtime.supervisor.app -> real UI/native/brain/audio workers
success_assertions: The platform source launcher uses a compatible provisioned Python, shows the real overlay, receives successful pings from four distinct worker processes, starts flows/hotkeys, exits cleanly, and leaves no managed process behind.
overrides.audit_note: Writable data, user state, add-ons, and the single-instance lock are isolated without replacing the production launcher or supervisor path.

overrides.production_entry_point: runtime.supervisor.app.main -> core.system.single_instance.acquire
success_assertions: When the application lock is already held, the second supervisor exits with the duplicate-instance code before constructing or starting any worker.
overrides.audit_note: The single-instance decision and supervisor entry are production code; the pre-held OS lock result is the deterministic boundary.

overrides.production_entry_point: First overlay frame -> UI-host fresh-install decision -> OnboardingWizard, plus production setup-check probe
success_assertions: A fresh state opens the real wizard after the overlay startup entry, completes setup, does not reopen once marked complete, and the production runtime/dependency checks return structured results for the installed environment.
overrides.audit_note: The source launcher dependency installer itself remains external to this UI workflow, but both the actual fresh-start gate and reusable dependency/runtime probes execute.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings Run profile setup button -> visible OnboardingWizard
success_assertions: A clean Settings dialog opens the real wizard through its visible button, while unsaved settings block the action; a completed wizard profile is persisted and visible after runtime reload.
overrides.audit_note: The earlier component-only audit was stale: the Settings entry button is now exercised directly.

overrides.production_entry_point: Onboarding Back and Continue buttons -> simple/advanced page sequence
success_assertions: Back returns from the name page to mode selection, Continue resumes it, advanced mode includes the provider page, and simple mode skips it without breaking later pages.
overrides.audit_note: Navigation is driven through real Qt buttons in both page sequences.

overrides.production_entry_point: Onboarding Wisp interface language combo -> live preview -> Finish setup persistence
success_assertions: Every interface-language option, including system default, survives Finish; every non-default choice invokes the live language preview route.
overrides.audit_note: All options exposed by LANGUAGE_OPTIONS are selected through the real combo.

overrides.production_entry_point: Onboarding Assistant response language combo -> Finish setup -> isolated settings file
success_assertions: System default, match-user, and every named assistant-language option persist exactly.
overrides.audit_note: All twelve values displayed by the wizard are exercised.

overrides.production_entry_point: Onboarding Theme combo -> live app-theme preview -> Finish setup persistence
success_assertions: System, Light, and Dark each persist; Light and Dark invoke their normal live-preview path while System remains the already-selected default.
overrides.audit_note: The palette renderer is the deterministic Qt boundary; selection and persistence are production.

overrides.production_entry_point: Advanced setup provider/model/endpoint/key controls -> keychain boundary and named profile persistence
success_assertions: Every provider offered by the wizard accepts an editable model; every key-backed provider routes the exact key name/value to secure storage; custom persists its HTTPS endpoint; Ollama correctly stores no key.
overrides.audit_note: All current _PROVIDER_DEFAULTS entries run through real controls; only the OS keychain write is captured.

overrides.production_entry_point: Onboarding Sign in with ChatGPT button -> browser OAuth callback/token check -> profile route
success_assertions: A successful sign-in changes the visible button to Connected and selects ChatGPT when no explicit provider was chosen; an explicit provider retains precedence; skipping sign-in performs no browser launch.
overrides.audit_note: The browser/token service is deterministic; button, polling state, and provider precedence are real.

overrides.production_entry_point: Onboarding reply-voice combo -> saved preference and optional staged installer request
success_assertions: None, local Kokoro, and cloud TTS each persist correctly across every STT choice; local routes to the correct Kokoro or combined installer plan.
overrides.audit_note: The complete TTS x STT Cartesian product is exercised through visible controls.

overrides.production_entry_point: Onboarding speech-input combo -> saved preference and optional staged installer request
success_assertions: None, local Whisper, and cloud/live voice each persist correctly across every TTS choice; local routes to the correct STT or combined installer plan.
overrides.audit_note: The complete TTS x STT Cartesian product is exercised through visible controls.

overrides.production_entry_point: Open a new chat after setup checkbox -> UI-host onboarding completion -> _show_chat(force_new=True)
success_assertions: Checked schedules exactly one forced-new chat after runtime apply; unchecked completes without requesting chat on Windows, macOS, and Linux guidance variants.
overrides.audit_note: Only the downstream chat window construction is captured in the UI-host chain; checkbox routing and Qt timer are production.

overrides.production_entry_point: Onboarding ready page -> platform-specific trial guidance label
success_assertions: Windows displays Ctrl+Q; macOS and Linux display Ctrl+Alt+Space; every variant also explains F9 voice and F7 read-aloud, independent of first-chat choice.
overrides.audit_note: The full 3-platform x 2-open-chat matrix is rendered by the real ready page.

overrides.production_entry_point: WispSupervisor.start_all
success_assertions: UI, native, brain, and audio workers start, answer ping, and remain isolated.
overrides.audit_note: Uses the real supervisor and four real worker subprocesses.

overrides.production_entry_point: OverlaySignals.set_state -> IconOverlay._on_state_changed -> floating/tray icon sprites
success_assertions: Idle, listening, thinking, and speaking each update the real floating icon through the production signal under both auto-hide states.
overrides.audit_note: The complete four-state by two-auto-hide matrix is exercised on real Qt widgets.

overrides.production_entry_point: floating icon Qt mouse press/move/release -> IconOverlay.eventFilter drag path
success_assertions: A real drag moves the floating icon and repositions its provider badge and context panel by the same delta.
overrides.audit_note: Synthetic Qt mouse events enter the exact installed event filter; no geometry helper is called directly.

overrides.production_entry_point: OverlaySignals.hide_icon -> icon backstop timer -> guarded auto-hide slot
success_assertions: Auto-hide keeps idle hidden, reveals every active state, starts the real backstop timer, and hides the icon when it expires.
overrides.audit_note: Auto-hide and state are tested as a full 2 x 4 behavior matrix.

overrides.production_entry_point: tray Hide/Show icon QAction -> IconOverlay._toggle_icon
success_assertions: Triggering the real tray QAction hides the icon, changes its label to Show icon, and restores the icon on the next trigger.
overrides.audit_note: The test triggers QAction.trigger rather than invoking the toggle slot.

overrides.production_entry_point: real UI-worker Last chat tray QAction -> show_last_chat signal -> QtProtocolHost._show_chat
success_assertions: The real Last chat action makes the production ChatWindow visible in a four-worker runtime.
overrides.audit_note: Runs for both external-provider modes through real IPC workers.

overrides.production_entry_point: real UI-worker Memory tray QAction -> supervisor FlowController.open_memory -> MemoryViewer
success_assertions: The Memory QAction crosses UI event IPC and the real brain memory-list call before the MemoryViewer becomes visible.
overrides.audit_note: Uses real UI and brain workers, not a signal recorder.

overrides.production_entry_point: real UI-worker Addon Manager tray QAction -> supervisor FlowController.open_addons -> add-on dialog
success_assertions: The Addon Manager QAction crosses UI event IPC and the real brain add-on-list call before the manager becomes visible.
overrides.audit_note: Uses real UI and brain workers, not the singleton helper alone.

overrides.production_entry_point: real UI-worker Settings tray QAction -> supervisor FlowController.open_settings -> SettingsDialog
success_assertions: The real Settings QAction crosses UI event IPC and makes the production SettingsDialog visible.
overrides.audit_note: The menu action itself is triggered inside the real UI worker.

overrides.production_entry_point: visible provider badge QPushButton -> IconOverlay._open_harness_controls -> HarnessControlsDialog
success_assertions: Clicking the real provider badge opens ChatGPT controls in Codex mode and Claude controls in Claude mode.
overrides.audit_note: Both behaviorally distinct provider badge modes run in separate real four-worker runtimes.

overrides.production_entry_point: real UI-worker Runtime Status tray QAction -> supervisor snapshot -> live runtime diagnostics dialog
success_assertions: The Runtime Status QAction crosses supervisor IPC and displays the live diagnostics window while all workers are active.
overrides.audit_note: This is the supported pure-Python UI-host path.

overrides.production_entry_point: real UI-worker Quit tray QAction -> QApplication.quit -> UI worker exit
success_assertions: Triggering the real Quit QAction exits the actual UI worker, after which supervisor shutdown leaves no remaining worker.
overrides.audit_note: The test observes the process exit rather than a mocked quit callback.

overrides.production_entry_point: sync_start_on_login -> Windows HKCU Run / macOS LaunchAgent / Linux XDG autostart entry
success_assertions: Every supported OS writes an enabled login entry with the Wisp launch command and removes it when disabled; source and packaged modes select their proven launch entries.
overrides.audit_note: OS persistence is isolated/faked only at the registry boundary; the command selection is production code and links to F001/F002 launcher acceptance.

overrides.production_entry_point: IntentOverlay(caller_idx=0) general picker construction/show
success_assertions: The production general picker is visible and all of its built-in action rows can be selected by key and click.
overrides.audit_note: Real Qt widgets and events are used.

overrides.production_entry_point: general IntentOverlay What is this row
success_assertions: The real row emits the exact configured What is this prompt by both input methods.
overrides.audit_note: Exact configured prompt and glyph are asserted.

overrides.production_entry_point: general IntentOverlay Explain simply row
success_assertions: The real row emits the exact configured Explain simply prompt by both input methods.
overrides.audit_note: Exact configured prompt and glyph are asserted.

overrides.production_entry_point: general IntentOverlay How do I fix this row
success_assertions: The real row emits the exact configured repair prompt by both input methods.
overrides.audit_note: Exact configured prompt and glyph are asserted.

overrides.production_entry_point: IntentOverlay(caller_idx=1) rewrite/paste picker construction/show
success_assertions: The production rewrite picker is visible and all of its built-in action rows can be selected by key and click.
overrides.audit_note: Real Qt widgets and events are used.

overrides.production_entry_point: rewrite IntentOverlay Fix grammar row
success_assertions: The real row emits the exact configured Fix grammar prompt by both input methods.
overrides.audit_note: Exact configured prompt and glyph are asserted.

overrides.production_entry_point: rewrite IntentOverlay Simplify row
success_assertions: The real row emits the exact configured Simplify prompt by both input methods.
overrides.audit_note: Exact configured prompt and glyph are asserted.

overrides.production_entry_point: rewrite IntentOverlay Improve tone row
success_assertions: The real row emits the exact configured Improve tone prompt by both input methods.
overrides.audit_note: Exact configured prompt and glyph are asserted.

overrides.production_entry_point: visible IntentOverlay.keyPressEvent -> assigned row glyph -> delayed _fire
success_assertions: Every general and rewrite built-in runs from its assigned single-key shortcut.
overrides.audit_note: Twelve total matrix selections cover key and click methods.

overrides.production_entry_point: visible IntentOverlay.mousePressEvent -> painted row hit-test -> delayed _fire
success_assertions: Every general and rewrite built-in runs by clicking its actual painted row.
overrides.audit_note: QTest clicks the row rectangle; the selection helper is not invoked directly.

overrides.production_entry_point: WispSupervisor.shutdown
success_assertions: Every worker process has exited after normal app shutdown.
overrides.audit_note: Normal shutdown is real; partial-failure shutdown remains secondary resilience evidence.

overrides.production_entry_point: native.hotkey event -> ui.intent.chosen event -> FlowController query stream
success_assertions: Streaming chunks reach the reply UI and the completed answer is added to chat.
overrides.audit_note: External provider and workers are deterministic fakes; the production flow controller and event path are real.
Brain cancellation works, but no user Stop/Cancel control currently drives this full chain in the acceptance test.

overrides.production_entry_point: Onboarding profile fields/buttons and Settings Save changes button
success_assertions: Two profiles persist independently and the selected profile reloads with its edited model.
overrides.audit_note: Uses visible Qt controls and a real isolated settings file.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings profile menu Rename profile action
success_assertions: The renamed profile label is visible and persisted.
overrides.audit_note: Uses the real menu action; only the modal text response is supplied by the test.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings profile menu Delete profile action
success_assertions: Deleting inactive and final profiles updates disk, active selection, and the visible profile label.
overrides.audit_note: Uses the real menu action; only the confirmation response is supplied by the test.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings Run profile setup button -> OnboardingWizard
success_assertions: Clean Settings opens the visible guided wizard; dirty Settings blocks it until pending changes are resolved.
overrides.audit_note: The Settings entry and both clean/dirty branches are real; existing profile workflows cover completed wizard persistence.

overrides.production_entry_point: SettingsDialog Save changes button click
success_assertions: Changed values are serialized, the callback identifies them, and a fresh dialog reloads them.
overrides.audit_note: Uses a real Qt button and real settings serializer against isolated disk state.

overrides.production_entry_point: Application/worker runtime log initialization
success_assertions: Runtime and worker stderr log files are created and contain emitted diagnostic text.
overrides.audit_note: Automatic operational feature; its real entry is lifecycle/log initialization rather than a button.

overrides.production_entry_point: Worker exit event and query failure event -> FlowController recovery
success_assertions: A dead brain worker is replaced and a failed query returns the UI to idle with a recovery notice.
overrides.audit_note: Automatic runtime behavior is exercised through real worker death and production flow events.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings navigation QListWidget mouse clicks
success_assertions: Every visible navigation row selects the corresponding real Settings page.
overrides.audit_note: All eight current pages are selected through Qt mouse input.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings search QLineEdit keyboard input
success_assertions: A matching query leaves only the owning page, a no-match query leaves none, and clear restores every page.
overrides.audit_note: Uses real keyboard events and visible page state.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings Cancel button mouse click
success_assertions: Cancel closes Settings and both clean and dirty cancellation leave the real settings file unchanged after reopen.
overrides.audit_note: Covers clean and unsaved-change states.

overrides.production_entry_point: Theme combo selection -> Settings Save changes button
success_assertions: System, Light, and Dark each persist, invoke theme application, and reload selected.
overrides.audit_note: Parameterized over every supported Theme mode.

overrides.production_entry_point: IntentOverlay custom shortcut, prompt editor keyboard input, and Enter
success_assertions: The exact typed prompt is emitted once through the production intent_chosen signal.
overrides.audit_note: Uses Qt keyboard events; downstream streaming is separately accepted by F049.

overrides.production_entry_point: Escape key on the visible IntentOverlay
success_assertions: Escape emits cancellation exactly once.
overrides.audit_note: Uses a real Qt key event.

overrides.production_entry_point: IntentOverlay production QTimer timeout
success_assertions: A positive configured timeout cancels an abandoned picker.
overrides.audit_note: Waits on the real Qt timer and cancellation signal.

overrides.production_entry_point: IntentOverlay with configured timeout zero
success_assertions: The picker remains visible past the positive-timeout window and still accepts manual Escape cancellation.
overrides.audit_note: Exercises the persistent and manual-close states.

overrides.production_entry_point: Full-screen mode click on the visible SnipOverlay
success_assertions: The production overlay emits the complete virtual-desktop region and no cancellation.
overrides.audit_note: Uses a real Qt mouse event.

overrides.production_entry_point: Escape key on the visible SnipOverlay
success_assertions: The focused capture overlay emits cancellation once.
overrides.audit_note: Uses a real Qt key event after the deferred focus sequence.

overrides.production_entry_point: caller hotkey -> selection request -> native await-selection boundary -> restored Intent picker
success_assertions: The newly selected text returns to the pending picker with an enabled token-estimated Selection chip and the draft preserved.
overrides.audit_note: The external OS selection adapter is fake; the Wisp flow and event transitions are real.

overrides.production_entry_point: default config -> hotkey registration spec -> native hotkey event -> read-aloud flow
success_assertions: F7 is the default, the action is registered, and its native event synthesizes the selected text.
overrides.audit_note: The OS hook and audio device are external deterministic boundaries.

overrides.production_entry_point: TTS speak-replies setting -> completed reply flow and manual read-aloud flow
success_assertions: A completed answer stays silent while manual selected-text read-aloud still synthesizes and plays.
overrides.audit_note: Uses production FlowController paths with provider/audio boundaries faked.

overrides.production_entry_point: enabled TTS speak-replies setting -> streamed query completion -> audio queue
success_assertions: Stable reply segments synthesize and play in order before and through final completion.
overrides.audit_note: Provider and audio playback are deterministic external boundaries.

overrides.production_entry_point: read-selection-aloud action -> native selection snapshot -> TTS synthesis/playback
success_assertions: Selected text is synthesized, played, displayed as reading, and no model query occurs.
overrides.audit_note: Native selection and audio device are external deterministic boundaries.

overrides.production_entry_point: voice press/release events with transcript review enabled -> Intent picker -> accepted query
success_assertions: The transcript opens for review before querying and the reviewed context choice reaches the query.
overrides.audit_note: Microphone/STT/UI processes are external worker boundaries; FlowController state is real.

overrides.production_entry_point: voice press/release events with review disabled -> transcription -> query stream
success_assertions: Recording starts/stops, transcript is shown and queried, and the reply persists to chat without review.
overrides.audit_note: Microphone/STT/provider are external deterministic boundaries.

overrides.production_entry_point: Intent shortcut File access combo Off -> Save changes -> config reload -> runtime tool policy
success_assertions: Off persists and grants none of the five local-file tools.
overrides.audit_note: Uses visible Settings controls, real env persistence, config reload, and production policy.

overrides.production_entry_point: Intent shortcut File access combo Read only -> Save changes -> config reload -> runtime tool policy
success_assertions: Read only persists and grants list/read while withholding create/edit/write.
overrides.audit_note: Uses visible Settings controls, real env persistence, config reload, and production policy.

overrides.production_entry_point: Intent shortcut File access combo Ask before writing -> Save changes -> config reload -> runtime tool policy
success_assertions: Ask persists, grants all five tools, and writes require an approval decision with a diff.
overrides.audit_note: Uses visible Settings, real persistence/policy, and the real approval/toolbox path.

overrides.production_entry_point: Intent shortcut File access combo Write automatically -> Save changes -> config reload -> runtime tool policy
success_assertions: Write automatically persists, grants all five tools, and each tool completes its disk operation.
overrides.audit_note: Uses visible Settings, real persistence/policy, and real isolated filesystem operations.

overrides.production_entry_point: live model-tool dispatcher list_files
success_assertions: The allowed folder listing contains the real seeded file.
overrides.audit_note: Uses the production dispatcher and a real isolated filesystem.

overrides.production_entry_point: live model-tool dispatcher read_file
success_assertions: The real seeded file content is returned.
overrides.audit_note: Uses the production dispatcher and a real isolated filesystem.

overrides.production_entry_point: live model-tool dispatcher create_file
success_assertions: A new file is created once with the requested content.
overrides.audit_note: Uses the production dispatcher and a real isolated filesystem.

overrides.production_entry_point: live model-tool dispatcher edit_file
success_assertions: The requested text replacement is applied to the real file.
overrides.audit_note: Uses the production dispatcher and a real isolated filesystem.

overrides.production_entry_point: live model-tool dispatcher write_file
success_assertions: The existing file is overwritten with the requested content.
overrides.audit_note: Uses the production dispatcher and a real isolated filesystem.

overrides.production_entry_point: IconOverlay construction and show-icon runtime signal
success_assertions: The real always-on-top icon surface is created, shown, and resized live for every tested icon-size state.
overrides.audit_note: Uses the production overlay and visible Qt surface in an offscreen desktop session.

overrides.production_entry_point: Theme color fields -> Settings Save changes -> config reload -> application stylesheet
success_assertions: Four distinct custom palettes persist and the active application stylesheet uses the saved background color.
overrides.audit_note: Exercises every theme color role through real fields and the production live-apply callback.

overrides.production_entry_point: Icon size field -> Settings Save changes -> UI-host reload -> existing IconOverlay
success_assertions: Every tested icon size persists and changes both dimensions of the already-running icon surface.
overrides.audit_note: Covers compact, normal, and large sizes through the real live-apply chain.

overrides.production_entry_point: Bubble width/lines/font fields -> Save -> UI-host reload -> existing SpeechBubble.apply_config
success_assertions: Compact through roomy geometry values persist and update the live bubble width, line capacity, and font.
overrides.audit_note: The four compound profiles exercise the shared geometry branch without replacing Wisp internals.

overrides.production_entry_point: Bubble color fields -> Save -> UI-host reload -> existing SpeechBubble.apply_config
success_assertions: Background including alpha, text, and spoken-word colors persist and become the live bubble QColor values.
overrides.audit_note: All three bubble color roles are asserted after the production live-apply callback.

overrides.production_entry_point: Wheel-scroll and snap-back checkboxes -> Save -> live SpeechBubble behavior flags
success_assertions: All four scroll-enabled x snap-enabled combinations persist and reach the live bubble.
overrides.audit_note: Uses the complete 2 x 2 boolean interaction matrix.

overrides.production_entry_point: Normal/held reveal-speed fields -> Save -> live SpeechBubble reveal mode
success_assertions: Normal and speed-boosted branches each use their saved WPM across low and high profiles.
overrides.audit_note: Asserts both production speed branches after real settings reload.

overrides.production_entry_point: Display/snap delay fields -> Save -> live SpeechBubble timers
success_assertions: Minimum, normal, and long delays persist, respect the 500 ms bound, and update the existing timers.
overrides.audit_note: Covers the input boundary and multiple valid timer states.

overrides.production_entry_point: App-language combo -> repeated Save changes -> live Qt translator and Settings navigation
success_assertions: System, English, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, Spanish, and French each persist and leave all eight Settings pages navigable.
overrides.audit_note: Exercises the complete 6-language x 8-page matrix through real combo, button, and navigation events.

overrides.production_entry_point: Assistant-language combo -> repeated Save changes -> config.get_system_prompt
success_assertions: Every offered reply-language state persists and produces the exact runtime language instruction, including match-user and system-default branches.
overrides.audit_note: The downstream provider request matrix is still pending; this accepts the real Settings-to-runtime-prompt behavior only.

overrides.production_entry_point: Three system-prompt editors -> Save changes -> config reload -> Settings reopen
success_assertions: Distinct Wisp, ChatGPT, and Claude prompts persist, reload independently, and reach their separate runtime config values.
overrides.audit_note: Conversation-engine routing interactions remain pending; persistence and runtime separation are accepted.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings Profiles menu Low setup QAction -> Save changes -> config reload -> Settings reopen
success_assertions: The built-in profile becomes visibly selected, persists as the active default profile, reaches runtime routing, and reopens with all three model routes.
overrides.audit_note: Low setup is the only built-in profile currently exposed by the production menu.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings Run setup check button -> core.setup_check.run_setup_check -> report dialog
success_assertions: Pass and warning check rows are rendered with their messages and recommendations while Settings remains usable.
overrides.audit_note: Host provider/microphone probes are deterministic external boundaries; the Settings action and report formatter are real.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings navigation row -> Reset Page button -> confirmation -> scoped env rewrite -> live reload
success_assertions: Each of the eight pages resets only its owned state, preserves every sentinel on other pages, reloads, and stays selected.
overrides.audit_note: Parameterized over every current Settings page, including the About no-setting branch.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings Reset All button -> confirmation -> keychain/OAuth cleanup -> env deletion -> config reload
success_assertions: The isolated settings file disappears, runtime defaults return, both credential entries are deleted, all three OAuth sessions clear, and Settings stays usable.
overrides.audit_note: Keychain and OAuth stores are external deterministic boundaries; destructive persistence is real but isolated.

overrides.production_entry_point: OptionalInstallDialog auto-start timer -> real QProcess child -> merged output/elapsed widgets
success_assertions: Success, resolver failure, and user-cancelled processes all stream a real package chunk while still running, update elapsed time, and mirror output to disk.
overrides.audit_note: The three terminal outcomes use the production child-process and Qt signal path rather than calling output handlers directly.

overrides.production_entry_point: Visible Cancel button -> OptionalInstallDialog.cancel -> real QProcess terminate -> finished handler
success_assertions: Clicking Cancel while the real installer is active terminates it, records a nonzero terminal result, disables Cancel, and displays the cancelled state.
overrides.audit_note: The same test compares cancellation with natural success and failure terminal branches.

overrides.production_entry_point: Visible Copy log button -> QApplication clipboard
success_assertions: After success, failure, and cancellation, clicking Copy log places the exact complete visible installer log on the real Qt clipboard.
overrides.audit_note: All terminal outcomes prove the control remains usable and copies retained rather than synthetic text.

overrides.production_entry_point: Visible Open log folder button -> QDesktopServices local-file URL boundary
success_assertions: After every terminal outcome, clicking Open log folder sends the exact retained-log directory to the operating-system URL boundary.
overrides.audit_note: Only the external desktop launch is captured; button routing, folder construction, and QUrl conversion are production code.

overrides.production_entry_point: Terminal finished handler -> enabled Close button -> QDialog close event
success_assertions: Close is disabled while each installer runs, enabled after success/failure/cancellation, and a real click hides the dialog.
overrides.audit_note: The outcome matrix proves the same invariant across every terminal branch.

overrides.production_entry_point: Successful staged status restart_apply -> visible Restart app now button -> QApplication restart boundary
success_assertions: Restart is offered and routed only for successful restart-required installs, never after failure or cancellation.
overrides.audit_note: The process-wide quit boundary is recorded so the test can continue; visibility and click routing are real.

overrides.production_entry_point: Failure/cancellation finished handler -> durable status and mirrored log files
success_assertions: A resolver failure retains its durable detailed status and output, while cancellation retains the streamed output and cancellation marker.
overrides.audit_note: Both failure classes named by the inventory are exercised with a real subprocess.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings About page construction -> core.updater.current_version -> Current version label
success_assertions: The About page displays the installed version before any update action and keeps it visible through every check result.
overrides.audit_note: The packaged result matrix re-creates the full Settings dialog for each check state.

overrides.production_entry_point: About Check for updates button -> background update worker -> GitHub-release result state
success_assertions: Available asset, newer version without a host asset, up-to-date, and service-error results each produce the correct status, button, and retry state.
overrides.audit_note: The network result is deterministic; the real Settings thread, Qt signal carrier, progress UI, and result state machine run unchanged.

overrides.production_entry_point: About update button check -> download/retry -> confirmation -> apply/retry -> app-exit boundary
success_assertions: A selected packaged release survives download failure, retries to a verified file, survives apply cancellation and helper failure, then reaches Applying and schedules app exit.
overrides.audit_note: Only release hosting and the detached apply helper are captured external boundaries; every visible state transition uses the production button.

overrides.production_entry_point: Source-checkout About Pull latest button -> background apply_repo_update -> Settings result
success_assertions: Updated, already-current, and origin-error results all show pulling progress then return to an enabled Pull latest action with the exact result.
overrides.audit_note: The repository remote is a deterministic boundary; checkout-mode selection, worker threading, and UI reporting are real.

overrides.production_entry_point: Updater check/download/apply/repo worker phases -> About status label and action state
success_assertions: Checking, downloading, applying, and pulling progress are visible while active; every error returns an appropriate retry action without losing valid state.
overrides.audit_note: Covers every phase that changes the shared update status/button pair.

overrides.production_entry_point: About Create crash report button -> core.crash_report.create_crash_report -> ZIP on disk
success_assertions: The actual Settings action creates a ZIP whose oversized log is limited to its final 512 KiB and whose log text has emails, API keys, and local paths redacted.
overrides.audit_note: The produced archive is opened and inspected rather than accepting a mocked path.

overrides.production_entry_point: Successful crash-report creation -> core.system.file_browser.reveal_path
success_assertions: The file-browser boundary receives the exact newly created ZIP and Settings displays its filename.
overrides.audit_note: Only Explorer/Finder launch is captured; report identity and success routing are real.

overrides.production_entry_point: Crash-report candidate-log collector and fixed metadata writer
success_assertions: Chat, memory, settings/environment, and keychain sentinels are absent from every archive member and the action never reads settings or secret storage.
overrides.audit_note: The test creates every excluded category beside eligible logs before clicking the production action.

overrides.production_entry_point: Successful crash-report action -> review-before-sharing confirmation message and user-openable ZIP
success_assertions: Settings identifies the exact saved archive and explicitly tells the user to review the ZIP; the same ZIP is successfully opened and audited by the test.
overrides.audit_note: Review is a user decision, so acceptance proves the archive and review surface exist and agree.

overrides.production_entry_point: Tray Runtime Status QAction -> UI-host event -> FlowController -> UI-host dialog and live publisher
success_assertions: Native, UI, brain, and audio running/stopped configurations render with PIDs; backlog and live events appear; refresh, copy, open-log-folder, and close controls all work.
overrides.audit_note: The production tray signal, supervisor handlers, runtime log, UI dispatch, and Qt window are linked in one process while only the folder launch is captured.

overrides.production_entry_point: Settings About Uninstall Wisp button -> run_uninstall_dialog -> exact-target critical confirmation
success_assertions: Release and source confirmations enumerate every exact target; No removes nothing, while Yes proceeds only with the displayed plan.
overrides.audit_note: Plans are built by the production safety planner entirely under pytest temporary roots.

overrides.production_entry_point: Confirmed uninstall plan -> rendered native helper -> isolated app/data/model target deletion
success_assertions: Confirmed release and source helpers remove temporary Wisp app trees, settings/chats/memory/add-ons/tools/logs/updates/optional-package data roots, and Wisp model cache targets; cancellation removes none.
overrides.audit_note: The native helper scripts really execute, but every target is an isolated fake Wisp installation below tmp_path.

overrides.production_entry_point: build_uninstall_plan frozen/source branch -> explicit confirmation -> native helper target list
success_assertions: The release plan excludes the source checkout; the source plan names and warns about its checkout, and deletes it only after that exact plan is confirmed.
overrides.audit_note: Both build kinds run through the same production Settings button and confirmation.

overrides.production_entry_point: Confirmed plan -> core.uninstaller.launch_uninstaller -> native wait/delete/self-remove helper
success_assertions: Packaged and source helpers wait for the supplied Wisp PID, delete their literal plan targets, remove their own script, and schedule Wisp exit after launch.
overrides.audit_note: Credential deletion and detached-process creation are deterministic boundaries; rendered helpers execute synchronously for proof and clean up isolated targets.

overrides.production_entry_point: native.hotkeys.start -> platform backend registration -> native.hotkey worker event -> native.hotkeys.stop
success_assertions: Windows RegisterHotKey, Linux pynput, and the macOS Carbon helper routes each start through the production native entry, deliver the registered action as a native.hotkey event, and cleanly release their backend.
overrides.audit_note: Only the actual OS registration/event source is deterministic; binding construction, backend selection, helper protocol, worker event conversion, and lifetime are real.

overrides.production_entry_point: native.selected_text -> macOS AX / Linux Wayland AT-SPI / Linux X11 PRIMARY / Windows UIA route
success_assertions: The public native selection entry returns the exact focused selection through all four supported accessibility routes without falling into clipboard injection.
overrides.audit_note: Accessibility frameworks and desktop selection owners are the external boundaries; the production platform decision tree runs unchanged.

overrides.production_entry_point: native.paste_text -> platform focus/selection target -> clipboard write -> paste -> optional original-clipboard restore
success_assertions: For Linux clipboard injection, Windows anchored UIA, macOS direct AX, and macOS clipboard injection, both restore-disabled and restore-enabled policies preserve the documented clipboard result.
overrides.audit_note: The 4 x 2 matrix proves the restore policy at every paste branch; direct AX correctly never reads or mutates the clipboard.

overrides.production_entry_point: native.paste_text -> previously captured PID or accessibility focus token -> target input method
success_assertions: Generated text is delivered through Linux focus plus paste, Windows cached UIA range, macOS cached AX element, and macOS app activation plus Cmd+V, under both clipboard policies.
overrides.audit_note: OS focus, clipboard, and synthetic-key calls are captured external boundaries; target selection and paste sequencing are production code.

overrides.production_entry_point: Visible Snip overlay area/app/full controls -> emitted capture region with live virtual-desktop origin and monitor DPR
success_assertions: Area drag, app target, and full virtual desktop produce usable regions; live negative/positive monitor origins and 1x/2x DPR changes are read at selection time, while macOS remains in logical points.
overrides.audit_note: The real Qt overlay and visible capture-mode controls run offscreen; only the physical monitor topology is modeled.

overrides.production_entry_point: macOS native worker route -> Carbon helper / AX selection / AX paste or activation-plus-Cmd+V
success_assertions: The helper process reports ready, forwards an event, and terminates; AX selection and in-place paste work, fallback activation plus Cmd+V works, and capture coordinates stay in macOS logical points.
overrides.audit_note: Carbon, ApplicationServices, Quartz, and AppKit are external OS boundaries; every Wisp helper/protocol and fallback decision is real.

overrides.production_entry_point: Linux native worker route -> pynput hotkeys / Wayland AT-SPI selection / X11 PRIMARY selection
success_assertions: Linux selects and starts its pynput backend, emits the registered event, reads focused text through both Wayland AT-SPI and X11 PRIMARY, and pastes to the captured external target.
overrides.audit_note: The display server and input hook are deterministic boundaries; Linux session routing and Wisp event/paste behavior are real.

overrides.production_entry_point: Windows native worker route -> RegisterHotKey / UIA selected range / clipboard and paste
success_assertions: Windows selects its RegisterHotKey backend, emits the registered action, reads UIA selection, reselects the captured UIA range before paste, obeys both clipboard policies, and translates live monitor origin/DPR changes.
overrides.audit_note: Win32/UIAutomation/clipboard calls are external boundaries; Wisp's anchored-control and multi-monitor logic remains real.

overrides.production_entry_point: Real top-level Qt surfaces -> shared window chrome and fit_window_to_screen -> title-bar controls
success_assertions: An oversized off-screen popup is fitted within the current screen, survives replacement after destruction, contains window-manager refusal, and exposes working minimize, maximize, and close controls.
overrides.audit_note: Real Qt top-level windows are shown offscreen; native window-manager move/pinning refusal is the captured desktop boundary.

overrides.production_entry_point: Visible log/report/task/add-on controls and conversation menu action -> shared OS reveal boundary
success_assertions: The actual Open-log, crash-report, task result/scope, Open add-ons, and Browse conversation actions route their exact existing targets; every file/folder x Windows/macOS/Linux case produces the correct native command.
overrides.audit_note: Qt controls and target selection are real; process creation for Explorer, Finder, or xdg-open is captured to keep CI deterministic.
